kept.text:
SHIB ON-CHAIN REPORT | April 27 – May 4, 2026

Market Overview
🟧 SHIB crosses a major adoption milestone while exchange activity remains active but balanced. Retail demand surged while whale positioning held steady.

1. Holder Milestone
SHIB reached 1,584,275 unique addresses, confirming continued expansion of the holder base.

2. Massive Monday Spike
April 27 delivered the strongest daily growth, driving a weekly total of ~24,000 new wallets.

3. Growth Phases
Early week growth ranged from +1,100 to +10,000 daily.
Late week stabilized to ~1,050 new holders per day.

4. Retail vs Whale Structure
Top 100 wallets still control ~61% excluding burn, showing whales are holding while retail enters.

5. Exchange Netflow
Arkham data shows a -131B SHIB net outflow from exchanges.
This signals movement toward self custody rather than selling.

6. High Volume Churn
Over 1T SHIB moved between wallets, largely identified as internal reshuffling, not real sell pressure.

7. Revolut & Binance Activity
Large transfers between these platforms were flagged as operational flows, not market exits.

8. Exchange Balances Snapshot
Binance holdings slightly increased to ~82.1T SHIB
Robinhood remains stable at ~54T SHIB
Crypto. com shows outflows toward cold storage
Top 10 wallets (~164T SHIB) continue consolidating

Key Insight

Retail adoption is accelerating again, reaching levels not seen since previous major cycles. At the same time, exchange reserves are thinning slightly, creating a more stable supply environment.

High activity without net selling pressure suggests repositioning rather than exit.

SHIB is expanding its base while holding structural strength.

kept.text:
Revolut is quietly building a full lending engine in Singapore — and it’s designed for the whole region. 

Revolut continues hiring for senior credit and risk roles in Singapore, signalling it is preparing to launch credit cards and unsecured loans locally by the end of 2026, with the city-state serving as the development hub for broader Asia-Pacific expansion.

Revolut is assembling a complete local credit function from the ground up: risk frameworks, policies, product design, and scalable infrastructure. 

This move comes as Revolut positions Singapore as its anchor for Southeast Asia, having already doubled headcount there in 2025 and planning to triple it to over 300 people in the coming years.   

Revolut has already filled up other senior credit roles, and now it is looking to hire a Retail Credit Risk Manager — responsible for designing a full local retail credit risk framework, including PD/ECL models, stress testing, and MAS Notice 635 compliance for credit cards, personal loans, and SME lending. 

Revolut's leaked pitch document, as reported by Sifted last year, laid out its plans: "Credit cards to launch in Mexico, Brazil, Australia, and Singapore by the end of 2026."   

Singapore is not just another market on that list. The Head of Lending job description states the team will "design, develop and launch credit products in Singapore... and then creating a local setup in each country with scalable risk management and portfolio management solutions."  

Revolut holds a Major Payment Institution licence in Singapore (payments and crypto only), but they don't have a credit licence. 

This suggests they prepare for either additional MAS approvals or structured bank partnerships to enable actual lending.  

By centralising product and risk development in Singapore, Revolut can move faster into other SEA markets while maintaining consistency — a classic hub-and-spoke model that has served revolut well in Europe. 

#Singapore

kept.text: Apart from the screen and the roof that’s not even our stadium

Turning Everyday Spending Into Bitcoin: A Closer Look at the @KoloHub Crypto Card

Holding crypto has long been about waiting and watching markets, tracking charts, and hoping for long-term appreciation. But the next evolution of digital assets is already here:
💪Using your crypto in daily life while continuing to grow your portfolio.

👉That’s where the Kolo Crypto Card is starting to stand out.

Spend Crypto Anywhere, Effortlessly

🔥The Kolo Card allows users to load and spend a wide range of cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), USDT, and even meme coins like PEPE. It’s designed to bridge the gap between digital assets and real-world usability.

Because it operates on the Visa network, it’s accepted in over 170 countries, meaning your crypto can be used almost anywhere traditional cards are accepted.

Seamless Payments together with Modern Convenience

👉The experience is built for speed and simplicity:

🔥Instant integration with Apple Pay and Google Pay
🔥Tap to pay functionality for everyday purchases
🔥Fast onboarding and streamlined KYC process

Whether it’s a coffee, groceries, subscriptions, or travel expenses, transactions feel just like using any standard debit card.

👉Earn Bitcoin While You Spend

One of the most compelling features is the 2% BTC cashback on purchases. Instead of spending and losing value, users accumulate Bitcoin with every transaction.

This turns routine expenses into a passive accumulation strategy, effectively “stacking sats” without changing spending habits. No Friction Crypto Utility

👉Kolo’s approach emphasizes:

🔥Minimal fees
🔥Fast transaction processing
🔥A smooth, user-friendly interface

The goal is clear: make crypto spending practical, not complicated.

👉 Ambassador Program

Beyond the card itself, Kolo is also building a community driven ecosystem through its Ambassador Program (Beta).

🔥 $15,000 monthly reward pool
Top 25 contributors earn based on content quality, reach, and credibility
Additional incentives for active users holding Kolo balances

This initiative blends content creation with real world incentives, encouraging organic growth and long-term engagement.

👉The Bigger Picture

The Kolo Crypto Card reflects a broader shift in the crypto space, from passive holding to active utility. As adoption grows, tools that seamlessly connect digital assets with everyday life will likely define the next phase of the industry.

For users looking to do more with their crypto while continuing to build their Bitcoin position, solutions like Kolo offer a practical and forward looking approach.

#Kolo #DebitCard
